title: Newly allocated loopback devices are not visible to privileged containers
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Podman does not seem to be able to see newly allocated loopback devices in privileged containers. Even with udev running in the container, the container inherits all of the existing loopback devices and fails to see any new ones allocated on the host.

strace shows repeated attempts to find the loopback device displayed by the command `losetup -f`, it is allocated on the host, but it is not visible to the container until a new container is built after it has been allocated.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `sudo podman run --privileged --name test -ti fedora /bin/bash`

2. `touch /tmp/filename`

3. `losetup -f /tmp/filename`

4. Repeat until all of the available loopback devices on the host are exhausted.

5. Check that there is one additional loopback device available on the host that is not visible to the container.

**Describe the results you received:**

`losetup: /tmp/testfilenext: failed to set up loop device: No such file or directory`

**Describe the results you expected:**

`losetup -f [FILENAME]` should succeed.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

I have reproduced this with SELinux completely disabled and both in environments running systemd/udev and not running systemd/udev.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
2.2.1
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
Actually, I wonder... We're not doing a proper `/dev/` mount, just a tmpfs on which we create nodes for every current device on the system, with `--privileged`. If `losetup` is making new nodes, they may only be appearing on the host namespace?

A bind-mount of `/dev/` into the container (`-v /dev:/dev --mount type=devpts,dest=/dev/pts`) may work better in that case?

@mheon Interestingly, docker has the same exact problem. While I'm curious why this occurs even running udev in the container (I was under the impression that that would create the device nodes in `/dev` for the container as well), using a bind-mount of `/dev` into the container seems to resolve the problem. Thanks!

Yes --privileged just turns off the security constraints and creates devices to match the host, it does not mount the /dev into the host, and udev inside of the container would not see what is going on outside of the container in a different mount namespace.
